pending.body: In `record_planned_elsewhere_mapping` (service.rs:2353), the docstring says 'If the secondary NDJSON write fails, the journal still contains the mapping for audit purposes' but the function propagates the NDJSON error via `?` at line 2390. This means callers see `Err` even though the authoritative journal record was successfully written. Either (a) downgrade the NDJSON failure to a warning log and return `Ok`, consistent with the stated 'secondary' semantics, or (b) update the docstring to say the function returns an error on NDJSON failure despite the journal succeeding. Currently all callers handle the error gracefully (best-effort paths), so this is a documentation/contract clarity issue, not a data-loss bug.

Fix `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1850-1893` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1896-1933` so planner/voter/arbiter prompts include the planned-elsewhere classification and `mapped_to_bead_id` for each amendment before `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1554-1590` uses that metadata to suppress restarts. Right now the panel only votes on the amendment body, so a reviewer can accept the concern without ever seeing or approving the 'planned elsewhere, mapped to bead X' claim that decides whether the active bead restarts.

Fix `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:733-763` so a planned-elsewhere mapping is not marked permanently verified until any posted `br comment` has also been durably flushed. As written, `verify_and_comment_mappings` reports `comment_posted` as soon as `br comment` returns `Ok` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:285-319`), `sync_flush()` failures are only logged, and the code still appends a verified mapping afterward. On replay that mapping is filtered out as already verified, so the comment can be lost permanently after a flush failure.

Fix `src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:82-85` so only true missing-bead exits are downgraded to a stale-warning fallback. The current blanket `Err(BrError::BrExitError { .. }) => not found` path treats every non-zero `br show` exit as definitive staleness, even though the rest of the codebase already distinguishes missing-bead errors from other `br` failures by inspecting the message (`src/cli/run.rs:314-327`). With the current logic, corrupt bead state or other `br` failures will be misreported as 'not found' and the active bead can proceed without a real verification.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 6 -> Plan and Implement
